meta.title: Fireside: Circle's Jeremy Allaire and Solana Policy Institute's Kristin Smith

transcript:
Kristin Smith: Good afternoon everyone. Um, you should definitely all buy Anthony's book and if you're not convinced, look at the back 'cause I wrote uh a little something on the back of the cover.

Jeremy Allaire: Nice.

Kristin Smith: Which was cool. So, um, but hopefully everyone in here is already a huge Solana fan. Um, I think this man needs no introduction. Jeremy Allaire with Circle, he's the Founder and CEO. They had the uh top performing IPO of the year, which is super exciting, not just for crypto but across the board. Um, and we only have a few minutes so we will dive right into the questions. Um, so it's been a big year. You IPO'd. We had the passage of the Genius Act. It seems now that something is is different. Um, are we at an inflection point? Have we hit our mainstream moment?

Jeremy Allaire: You know, this is uh... it's a question that I've been asked for about 12 years. Like, when is this going to be mainstream? Um, and uh, and along the way, um, I've always talked about, you know, several things that were necessary. Um, so some of it is technology, some of it is product execution, some of it is is is legal basically. And, um, I think, you know, those three were, hey, we need the technology to actually be at a point where it can scale, where it can support great user experiences, where in some ways the the the crypto infrastructure of it all could move into the background and people just experience better utility. And, um, and my view was actually in particular over the last year or two, like we were hitting that. We're and we're increasingly hitting that. And so that was like very positive. Not 100% there, but really making great progress. Um, and so that's sort of the infrastructure and UX side. But the the third the third piece was we need to kind of move this new system of monetary infrastructure and new literally a new form factor of money to be legal and to legalize it and enshrine it in the laws of the financial system everywhere in the world. And now we've done that. And and that is just truly extraordinary. And, I mean the Genius Act itself uh was five years of work as you know. The stablecoin bill uh was like five years of work. And and what landed is actually pretty close but like obviously some some some changes along the way. But that's in incredible moment. And so we now have this kind of um legal framework and, you know, covered stablecoins like USDC can be treated essentially as a new form of M1 electronic money in the dollar global financial system. Fully reserved, very safe, uh with I think the right kind of of safeguards around them, supervised by national banking regulators and and the like. So all of this combined, um, those were the things that I was always looking for and we hit those. And I think the market was experiencing its own organic growth and obviously Circle's IPO I think was a catalyst in in other ways. And all of a sudden like, yes, this is very clearly the inflection point that we've all been waiting for. And for, you know, for us it's been this uh extraordinary expansion of major companies that are actually now trying to build and invest in this. There's still lots of work to do. But like the other point though is that, well what does it mean to have an inflection point to the mainstream is that right now the market for digital assets, stablecoins, tokenization, you know, smart contract based finance and applications is tiny. It's a tiny, tiny, tiny little market. And the TAM is obviously extraordinary. And not only is the existing TAM extraordinary, but the internet tends to make TAMs multiply. And so the multiplier effect of this is is huge. And so I I as I said to every investor that we talked to when we went public is Circle's an early stage company. This is an early stage market. You need to be thinking about this over the next 10 to 15 years at a minimum. Uh, and that's and that's exciting. That's like for anyone here who's building, like that's incredibly exciting to know that we have that in front of us.

Kristin Smith: No, I think um you and I were both at the White House for the signing ceremony of the Genius Bill.

Jeremy Allaire: Yes.

Kristin Smith: And I think it was actually six years in the making because Congress started talking about it when Libra...

Jeremy Allaire: Yeah. I mean that was the genesis of the the Libra hearings. Yes, yes. But we actually built something.

Kristin Smith: It takes a lot of time uh in in the US to do that. But, um, but no I do think that was a critical moment. Um, but obviously we have things going on in the EU, um here in the UAE there have been a lot of progress on the regulatory front. Do you think in sort of a post-Genius world that we've kicked off a global race? 'Cause there is a natural tension there um, you know, sort of between the dollar being exported around the world and what other countries might do. How are you looking at it from a global perspective?

Jeremy Allaire: Yeah, I mean it's fascinating. Um, I I never thought that, you know, building a business in this spa- well, building a business in this space has this intersection of geopolitics and geoeconomics as well as changing law and fascinating technology and that's pretty it's it's pretty interesting. But, you know, I thought for a long time that eventually when this sort of got to this moment, right, that governments around the world were gonna were gonna wake up and be like, whoa, what does this mean for us? And I think it's similar to other epochs of the internet where, you know, kind of the internet as an infrastructure swept over communications and media and content and software and all and commerce and all these other industries. And now it's sweeping over the financial system and governments are having to react. And um there's geopolitical considerations, there's monetary and fiscal policy considerations, and then there's just how do you how do you regulate the sector. But I do think it is now accelerating. We're seeing um, you know, more governments at every level, like every every government in the world frankly is now actively working on policy in this space. Actively trying to figure out how to deal with dollar stablecoins, how to deal with local stablecoins, how to deal with digital assets more broadly, how to make it work with the fact that this is an internet scale phenomenon. This is not, you know, there's not the the Kazakhstan blockchain and the Kazakhstan thing. Everyone's in a global economic system. And so I I think it's an exciting time to work through those issues but I think, you know, it's um it's gonna it's gonna require some very significant changes in fiscal and monetary policy for countries around the world over the long run.

Kristin Smith: Um, I want to talk about Solana for a second because we're at a Solana conference.

Jeremy Allaire: Yes.

Kristin Smith: Um, I I think I've told you this before but it was actually your son uh who I was working with in my prior life at the Blockchain Association who every day would come to the office and talk about Solana. Which I assume he got from you.

Jeremy Allaire: He was getting it from me. Yes. Yes. Yes.

Kristin Smith: I don't know he got that from you. Uh, but he he was a huge Solana bull. I should have listened to him back then.

Jeremy Allaire: It was a dollar back then.

Kristin Smith: It was a dollar back then. Yes. Um, but, you know, Circle was very early in building on Solana. Um, and I think, you know, you've been one of the big advocates for the ecosystem. Um, where is Circle today with Solana and where are you guys going in the future?

Jeremy Allaire: Yeah, I mean, look, I mean we we um uh we we got introduced to the team um by by the Multicoin guys. Uh, thank you. Um, and and got to know Toly and Raj very early. And and actually it was right at that time it was in it was in um uh 2000 um uh yeah I think it was in 2000 um and we were just starting to think about how to bring USDC onto other chains. And we were very focused on, you know, to the other comment, like how do we get on a higher performance, more scalable infrastructure that's like made for financial applications, payment applications, etc. And we were super impressed uh with uh with with what we were seeing and and the and the team and the speed and everything. And we had one other chain that we launched before Solana but we we had had a contract in place to do that and so we did it. But like Solana was the next one. And I would say, you know, um it's been a huge investment theme for us and I I know we work with a lot of projects in the ecosystem uh as well. And, you know, I I would say, you know, when you look at on-chain transaction volume of USDC, actually the on-chain transaction volume on Solana dominates all other chains. And um the the maturation of the infrastructure is just making this more and more compelling. We're seeing more and more major consumer facing and merchant facing platforms adopting USDC on Solana as a primary infrastructure. So, I mean, we're we're thrilled with what's happened. I mean look at this. Like this is pretty awesome. Um, and um and I think, you know, for us, you know, we keep launching more and more infrastructure. There's obviously native USDC. We're building more and more protocols for cross-chain infrastructure. CCTP becomes a really key infrastructure for how liquidity can move between different networks and Solana. Um, wh- which is important. We have, you know, wallet developer tools and other things for Solana devs. Um, and we actually we have something that we've talked about uh coming soon um which is something called Gateway. And and Gateway I think is going to be really, really powerful. It's a permissionless protocol that will run on Solana. Lets people have essentially a unified balance where they have essentially a USDC balance and they don't need to think about like what chain it's on. And uh it may be if you're in like a Solana wallet or whatever you can hold it, but if you're in a wallet that has dif- different support for different networks, um you don't actually have to have balances on different networks anymore. You can just have a single unified balance and then you can do uh sends and spends to any network on Gateway with uh with minting on the other network in less than a second. And so to the UX problem and to dealing with capital efficiency and cross-chain liquidity, it's it's a huge step. And so that's that's something we're rolling out soon and and actually when that rolls out it gives us tools to build deeper commercial partnerships with um firms and projects in the Solana ecosystem. So stay stay uh stay uh watching for that.

Kristin Smith: Um, final question. We only have two minutes left. Uh, you've spoken a lot about the intersection of blockchain and AI. What is your vision there?

Jeremy Allaire: Yeah, I mean there's a there's obviously a lot of experimentation happening with agentic payments and sort of enabling AI to con- consume and transact with stablecoins. Obviously doing that on a uh an infrastructure like Solana is very great because of the speed and capital efficiency and and uh and and low fees. Um, but I would step back and just sort of say, you know, wh- what what I'm doing at least and we're doing at Circle is we're thinking about three to five years from now. In three to five years, if you look at the arc of what's happening with uh AI and you just kind of continue to extrapolate that over three to five years, it's it's not unrealistic to imagine that in in that period that there will be hundreds of millions if not billions of agentic s- you know agents basically wor- working uh continuously on behalf of people and institutions. And there'll be institutions that are all operated by AI as well. And in a world like that, um we need new we need new infrastructure models to to support the economic activity of that. And so AI AI infrastructure, AI agents and the like will be performing more and more of the economic activity of the world. And so we need to marry that with im- blockchain network infrastructure like Solana. And um so I I just my own view is like we need to be designing towards that future. Um, and um this isn't just about like how do you do high frequency trading better. Uh, this is about like all economic activity in the world coming on ch- coming on-chain in a world where, you know, effectively uh AI is is intermediating and executing uh a huge amount of work. And last thing I'll say with 10 seconds left is um the internet as new infrastructures and new utilities come online has has a a tendency to unlock kind of multiple orders of magnitude increases in output in whatever the thing it is. Whether that's communications or information published or media or uh or software published. And and my own view is that this is going to lead to probably, you know, many many orders of magnitude, maybe a million X increase in the amount of transactions that happen in the world. And we need to get ready for that. So.

Kristin Smith: Well, I know it's been a wild ride over the past several years uh but it sounds like it's going to be incredibly interesting going forward. Um, congratulations on your milestones this year and thank you for joining us at Breakpoint today.

Jeremy Allaire: Awesome. Thank you Kristin. Thanks everyone.

enrichment.generated_query: USDC Circle Solana Breakpoint 2025 Official Announcement Blog Technical Docs

tags.impact_reason: High-profile discussion between industry leaders regarding the regulatory maturity and institutional acceptance of stablecoins, specifically USDC, though the format is reflective rather than a new product launch.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a monolithic 'Gateway' as the Semantic Anchor because the story is explicitly about Circle's new 'Gateway' protocol. This physical metaphor represents the unification of disparate, fragmented 'cross-chain' liquidity streams into a single, coherent entity. For the homepage text, I integrated '<1s' as a holographic readout on the gateway itself. This is diegetic and highlights the protocol's key institutional benefit: sub-second settlement, which is a major technical achievement mentioned in the standfirst.
visual_idea.description: In a vast, minimalist hall reminiscent of a futuristic data center, a massive, monolithic gateway made of brushed aluminum and dark, semi-translucent crystal stands as the central focus. From the deep, hazy background, multiple separate streams of colored light (representing fragmented liquidity from different blockchains) converge on the structure. As they pass through the arch, they are seamlessly fused into a single, powerful, stable beam of brilliant cyan light that projects towards the viewer. Etched into the side of the aluminum frame, a small, clean holographic display glows with the text '<1s', indicating the settlement speed.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, ultra-photorealistic digital render of a vast, minimalist hall, resembling a futuristic data center. The central focus is a massive, monolithic gateway, towering high, crafted from exquisitely detailed brushed aluminum and dark, semi-translucent crystal that catches and refracts light. From the deep, hazy background, multiple distinct streams of vibrant colored volumetric light, representing fragmented liquidity from different blockchains, converge dynamically towards the monolithic structure. As these fragmented light streams pass through the gateway's arch, they seamlessly fuse into a single, intensely powerful, stable beam of brilliant cyan light, dramatically projecting outwards towards the viewer, cutting through the atmospheric haze. Etched precisely into the side of the brushed aluminum frame, a small, clean holographic display glows with the clearly visible text '<1s', indicating the instantaneous settlement speed. The composition is vertical (3:4 aspect ratio), emphasizing the immense height and epic scale of the gateway, with significant negative space at the top, creating a sense of grandeur and monumental presence. The lighting is cinematic with high dynamic range, highlighting realistic materials, intricate architectural details, and dramatic volumetric effects. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, cinematic contrast.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a monolithic 'Gateway' as the Semantic Anchor because the story is explicitly about Circle's new 'Gateway' protocol. We see the final moments of several distinct colored light streams as they are woven together and transmuted into a singular, intensely bright cyan beam. The intricate, geometric patterns of the crystal refract the light, while the brushed aluminum frame of the gateway is barely visible at the edges, providing textural contrast. The focus is on the fusion of energy and the pristine material science of the structure.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, detailed shot with symmetrical composition, central focus, and tight framing, filling the frame with intricate textures. Focus on the point of convergence within the gateway's crystalline interior, showcasing the final moments of several distinct colored light streams as they are woven together and transmuted into a singular, intensely bright cyan beam. The intricate, geometric patterns of the crystal refract the light with stunning precision, creating dynamic light play, while the brushed aluminum frame of the monolithic gateway is barely visible at the edges, providing a sharp textural contrast with its polished metallic surface. The entire scene emphasizes the fusion of energy and the pristine material science of the structure, illuminated by dramatic volumetric lighting and global illumination, with a high dynamic range. 8K, ultra-detailed, hyperrealistic, photorealistic, V6 realism, Octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), cinematic contrast.

editorial.headline: Circle Unveils ‘Gateway’ Protocol to Unify Cross-Chain USDC Liquidity
editorial.standfirst: Liquidity fragmentation is facing its end-game as Circle leverages Solana to abstract blockchain complexity. The new "Gateway" protocol promises institutions a singular, unified USDC balance that executes cross-chain settlements in under one second.
key_takeaways: Circle introduces "Gateway," a permissionless protocol on Solana to enable unified USDC balances across disparate chains.
USDC on-chain transaction volume on Solana now dominates all other networks combined.
The passage of the "Genius Act" officially classifies covered stablecoins as M1 electronic money, following Circle's market-leading IPO.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The era of managing fragmented stablecoin balances across disparate networks is concluding. **Circle** has confirmed the imminent rollout of "Gateway," a permissionless protocol anchored on **Solana**. This infrastructure layer allows users and institutions to maintain a single, unified USDC balance while executing sends and spends across any supported network. By utilizing Solana’s high-throughput environment, Gateway achieves cross-chain minting and settlement in under one second, effectively rendering the underlying blockchain invisible to the end-user and solving the capital inefficiency that has long plagued cross-chain DeFi.

This technical leap aligns with a decisive shift in market dominance. **Circle** data reveals that on-chain transaction volume for USDC on **Solana** now eclipses that of all other chains. This is not merely a retail phenomenon; major consumer and merchant-facing platforms are increasingly defaulting to Solana as their primary settlement rail. The introduction of Gateway serves to cement this position, providing the tools necessary for deeper commercial partnerships that require the speed of a centralized database with the settlement finality of a blockchain.

The structural maturation of the market is underpinned by the recently enacted "Genius Act," which codifies covered stablecoins like USDC as M1 electronic money within the global dollar financial system. Following **Circle's** IPO—the top-performing public listing of the year—this regulatory clarity has triggered an inflection point for institutional entry. The focus has shifted from speculative experimentation to integrating stablecoins as core monetary infrastructure, supervised by national banking regulators.

Looking toward the medium term, the convergence of AI and blockchain dictates the next phase of scale. With the rise of agentic economies, where AI agents independently execute transactions, the demand for low-fee, high-velocity settlement will skyrocket. **Circle** projects a potential "million-X" increase in global transaction volume over the next decade, driven by these automated economic actors. Gateway is designed as the prerequisite rail for this future, ensuring that as AI-driven economic activity explodes, the liquidity powering it remains unified and accessible.
